focus_points.label: Workshop planning and lead time clarity.
focus_points.reason: Notes indicate workshops are planned last minute. This could be a symptom of unclear priorities or a lack of foresight in scheduling — worth exploring how planning can be more proactive.

focus_points.label: Current workload balance and stressors.
focus_points.reason: Last-minute planning might signal workload mismanagement or external pressures. Could be a capacity issue or a prioritization challenge — worth clarifying.

focus_points.label: Energy levels and motivation recently.
focus_points.reason: Feeling off about planning might correlate with energy or motivational dips. Could be burnout signals or just a temporary stress — worth checking in.

focus_points.label: Support needed for smoother workshop execution.
focus_points.reason: If planning is consistently last minute, there might be a need for more support or resources. Understanding what she needs could lead to better outcomes.

focus_points.label: Clarity on her role and expectations.
focus_points.reason: No specific signal — selected because this is a normal Something feels off topic for a Senior Acting Coach. Understanding if there's ambiguity in her role could be insightful.

brief.coreIssue: As a Senior Acting Coach, Taya appears to be facing challenges with last-minute workshop planning, which could stem from unclear priorities or a mismanaged workload. This 1:1 aims to explore whether her current planning process is a result of external pressures, capacity issues, or simply a lack of clarity in her role and expectations. Understanding these factors will help determine what support is necessary for more proactive and effective workshop execution.
brief.openingQuestion: Taya, can you walk me through how you currently plan your workshops and any challenges that might be affecting your lead time?
listenFor: whether she feels overwhelmed by her current workload
if they believe additional resources could improve planning
whether unclear role expectations contribute to last-minute planning
avoid: do not assume last-minute planning is due to lack of effort
do not suggest solutions before understanding her perspective
brief.goodOutcome: You will understand the root cause of last-minute workshop planning and identify if Taya needs additional support or resources.
brief.suggestedAction: Clarify and agree on a structured planning process that allows for more lead time and reduces last-minute stress.

queueRef.label: Next steps clarity
queueRef.name: What do you see as the next steps to improve the situation with the BAs, if any?
queueRef.description: Closes the session by identifying potential next steps or actions regarding the BA situation.
